summary: Backend testing of P1 features + new SaaS endpoints. 17/17 new-feature tests pass against the public ingress URL. Validated: brute-force lockout, async campaign send, AI lead scoring, lead detail+communications, AI auto-reply, integrations CRUD (no token leakage), daily briefing, plus regression on auth/me, dashboard, leads, campaigns, approvals.

minor.issue: Brute-force lockout uses identifier f"{request.client.host}:{email}" — behind k8s ingress request.client.host can rotate across ingress pods, so the per-IP counter fragments. In live testing, lockout triggered between attempts 5–10 instead of strictly on the 6th. Recommendation: trust X-Forwarded-For (first hop) OR fall back to email-only counter as a safety net so account always locks at exactly 5 fails.

Old regression test in /app/backend/tests/test_zeromark_backend.py::test_send_campaign_after_approval still asserts response key 'sent' which no longer exists (response is now {success,queued,message}). Old test should be updated to match new async contract — covered correctly in new test_zeromark_p1_features.py.

FastAPI BackgroundTasks runs in-process; if the worker is killed mid-send the campaign stays in SENDING forever (no recovery sweep). For production reliability consider a proper task queue (Celery/RQ/Arq) or a startup recovery pass that re-queues SENDING campaigns older than X minutes.

Sanitization is correct (returns only connected+label), but POST stores arbitrary config dict including secret tokens in plaintext in MongoDB. Should encrypt sensitive fields (account_sid/access_token) at rest or store only references to a secret manager.

action_items: Harden brute-force lockout: read X-Forwarded-For for real client IP OR add an email-only counter so 5 wrong attempts always locks regardless of source IP.
Update legacy test_zeromark_backend.py::test_send_campaign_after_approval to assert {success:true, queued:true} instead of 'sent' (campaign send is now async).
Add a startup recovery routine to re-process or fail any campaign stuck in SENDING beyond a timeout (BackgroundTasks is in-process and not durable).
Encrypt integration secrets (account_sid/auth_token/oauth tokens) at rest in db.integrations.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1340 lines — split into routers (auth, leads, campaigns, ai, integrations, briefing, subscription) for maintainability (>700 line threshold).
Brute-force key {ip}:{email} unreliable behind reverse proxy; the protection is partially defeated in this deployment.
BackgroundTasks for campaign delivery is not durable. A pod restart leaves campaigns stuck in SENDING. No recovery cron/sweep present.
ai_draft_reply / score_leads_batch / generate_briefing each instantiate a new Groq client per call — fine, but consider a module-level client to avoid repeated TLS handshakes.
Login attempts collection has no TTL index → grows forever. Add TTL on last_attempt or locked_until.
Integrations stored config is not encrypted; secret tokens (Twilio SID, OAuth tokens) live in plaintext in MongoDB.
_run_campaign_send loads ALL leads (limit 2000) into memory and runs synchronous SMTP/Twilio sends in thread executor — fine for low volume, but no rate limiting and no retries on failure.
Briefing endpoint always inserts a new record on every call — no debounce; a misbehaving client could spam Groq.

success_rate.backend: 100% (17/17 new feature tests; 36/37 legacy — 1 legacy failure is intended contract change)

test_credentials: admin@zeromark.ai / admin123 (auto-seeded). Test users created on the fly with TEST_brute_*, TEST_reset_*, TEST_p1_* prefixes.
seed_data_creation: TEST_P1 lead created and deleted within the same session via fixture; brute-force test users remain in db.users (small footprint, prefixed TEST_).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_zeromark_p1_features.py going forward. Note: brute-force test allows up to 12 attempts because behind the ingress request.client.host rotates, fragmenting the per-IP counter — see action item to fix. Background campaign send takes up to ~40s to reach terminal state (admin has no leads → goes SENT immediately because sent>0 path is false; status becomes FAILED with sent_count=0). All Groq endpoints (score-batch, ai-reply, briefing/generate) cost 2-8s each.
rca of the issue: Brute-force lockout NOT firing on the 6th attempt deterministically: server uses identifier=f"{request.client.host}:{email}" but request.client.host returns the IP of the immediate downstream proxy (an ingress controller pod), which rotates across requests. The per-(IP,email) counter therefore fragments into multiple buckets and never reaches 5 within the same bucket. Reproduction: register a fresh user, hit POST /api/auth/login with wrong password 6 times — observed status sequence [401x6] in one run, [401x6,429,429,401,401] in another. Mitigation: extract real client IP from X-Forwarded-For header (left-most non-private IP) before composing the identifier, OR keep an additional email-only counter so the account locks at 5 wrong attempts regardless of source IP.
